items.title: A planning rule wouldn't bend so the lights at this Canberra oval had to

children.content: If the six light poles skirting Manuka Oval look like they're on a lean, it's because they are. The 47-metre-high poles slant inwards because otherwise they couldn't be approved under an unusual Canberra planning rule.

gallery.altText: Very large curved floodlights stand above a major sportsground.

thumbnail.altText: Very large curved floodlights stand above a major sportsground.

items.title: Woman charged for trying to print child abuse material at Canberra store

children.content: A NSW woman is arrested in Canberra after staff at a photo printing business allegedly discovered child abuse material she tried and failed to print due to a machine malfunction.

image.altText: Badge on the sleeve of Australian Federal Police officer's uniform.

items.title: Canberra man jailed over sexual abuse of 'extremely vulnerable' child

children.content: A man who sexually assaulted a 12-year-old girl after a "chance encounter" at a Canberra shopping centre has been jailed, with the judge finding he took advantage of an "extremely vulnerable" child.

image.altText: ACT Magistrate Court

items.title: Man accused of ramming, shooting at car in Canberra's north

children.content: The 20-year-old's defence lawyer said his client was needed at a barber shop, but Magistrate James Stewart said his circumstances did not meet the threshold for bail.

image.altText: A police car with lights on at night.

items.title: Victim of suspicious unit fire in Canberra identified

children.content: ACT Policing is treating the death of Kylee McHugh, 46, as a homicide and calling for people to come forward if they had recently seen her.

image.altText: A women takes a selfie.

items.title: ACT government's coercive control legislation won't lead to any charges for years

children.content: The government's bill does not detail specific coercive control behaviours which it says is deliberate.

items.title: Wheel falls off Link Airways flight during take-off from Canberra Airport

children.content: Damage to bearing parts meant investigators could not determine what caused a wheel to separate from a Link Airways-operated flight departing Canberra for Newcastle.

image.altText: A plane's wheels, with one wheel missing.

items.title: Canberra man becomes first person in ACT charged with exit trafficking

children.content: The man, who left his wife in India and did not return for the birth of his son, is the first person in the ACT to be charged with the crime.

image.altText: A de-identified man walks between two police officers.

items.title: Anneke French gave birth as smoke hung in the theatre. Did it hurt her baby?

children.content: Kelly’s son and ex-husband died "stupid" deaths in the Australian outback. She’s on a mission to make sure no more lives are lost.

gallery.altText: A white car driving in the middle of the bush.

items.title: Wide-open miss leaves Socceroos trailing Mexico in last chance to impress

children.content: Australia's World Cup hopefuls get one final chance to impress before the Socceroos' squad for the 2026 World Cup is finalised against Mexico at the Rose Bowl. Follow live.

image.altText: Mo Toure looks sad during a game for the Socceroos.

items.title: Dozens of arrests during Champions League celebrations on Paris streets

children.content: Celebrations of Paris Saint-Germain's Champions League victory have turned violent, as fans clashed with police on the streets of the French capital.

image.altText: Police are silhouetted as a firework explodes on a Paris street.

items.title: Surrounded by smoke in pregnancy, mothers fear it made their babies sick

children.content: Years after bushfires covered their cities in smoke, mothers are left wondering if their children's chronic illnesses began with what was in the air before they were born.

items.title: Police beat a woman in a Sydney street. Their own camera captured their cruelty

children.content: The family of a woman brutally assaulted by police in Sydney has fought to have body-worn camera vision released, in the hope officers will be better trained in responding to acute mental health situations.

image.altText: A still from a body-worn camera as a plain clothes officer draws his leg back to kick a naked woman on a street.

items.title: As One Nation broadens its economic appeal, the Liberals return to culture wars

children.content: The Coalition's answer to the rise of conservative populism has been to treat it primarily as a cultural phenomenon. Under Angus Taylor, that instinct appears to be hardening.

image.altText: An older man wearing a suit gives an impassioned speech.

items.title: Flotilla activists take evidence of alleged abuse to international court

children.content: Eleven Australians detained by Israeli forces while attempting to deliver aid to Gaza have submitted evidence to the International Criminal Court alleging abuse, torture and other violations during their detention.

items.title: Thousands without power after intense storm lashes WA

children.content: Thousands of homes are without power across Western Australia's south including Perth, after an intense storm crossed the coast overnight but residents have been warned the worst of the weather is yet to come.

items.title: How clay-court chaos is a return to the norm at the French Open

children.content: All the key favourites are now out of the men's draw at the French Open, but then again, this tournament has always been just a little bit different.

items.title: The end came quickly for Madeleine Ogilvie, who jumped before being pushed

children.content: It started with a seemingly innocuous question. Over the course of several months, Madeleine Ogilvie had many opportunities to provide clarification, but didn't until last week. Then the end came quickly.

items.title: At 15 Mohamed Semra made headlines. At 27 he’s making history

items.title: Police find 11yo boy who went missing in wild weather in Perth

children.content: An 11-year-old autistic boy, who is non-verbal, has been found after going missing from Perth's western suburbs in wild weather on Saturday afternoon.

items.title: 'Losing a generation': Why some countries are moving fast on AI in schools

children.content: Countries in Asia are emerging as the world's early adopters of AI in education, as the global race to lead in the technology accelerates.

items.title: AUKUS partners unveil plan to develop underwater drones

children.content: The US, Australia and United Kingdom have unveiled a new "signature" project to develop cutting edge weapons systems and sensors for underwater drones as they try to reinvigorate the second pillar of the AUKUS agreement.

items.title: This preacher was once part of NZ's gang world. Now he's trying to heal it

children.content: In a country where there now more gang members than police officers, a former gang member turned Christian pastor is trying to turn things around for others by addressing the root causes of gang life.

items.title: Unlikely material behind Whitsundays artist's fashion collection

children.content: In the front yard of her home in the picturesque Whitsundays, Felicity Chapman strips the last pieces of flesh and sinew from a strip of barramundi skin to make wearable leather art.

items.title: How Tiananmen Square vigils in Hong Kong have become unlawful

children.content: In May, Hong Kong's High Court began hearing closing arguments in the case of two democracy activists charged with inciting subversion for organising a candlelight vigil to remember Tiananmen Square.

items.title: Book tells story of Timorese asylum seekers' daring escape to Darwin

children.content: Jose da Costa's escape from Indonesian-occupied East Timor in 1995 has been detailed in a new book launched at the Northern Territory Writers Festival this weekend.

image.altText: A Timorese man, smiling, black curly short hair, light olive polo shirt, coastal background.

items.title: After nearly 80 years, the actors in this film have finally been understood

children.content: The "huge swathes" of Pitjantjatjara lines in the 1949 film Bitter Springs are at last translated.

items.title: Bioremediation of Agent Orange chemicals made in Sydney for Vietnam War

children.content: More than 50 years after the Vietnam War, students are working to remove Agent Orange remnants from a waterway connected to Sydney Harbour, where the chemicals were manufactured.

image.altText: A black and white photo of trees which appear to be dead with rubbish nearby the water with factories seen in the distance.
